data.title: Wireless and mobile charging join new EV standard
data.description:
The new standard also covers battery swapping for heavy vehicles.

Singapore has updated its national standard for electric vehicle (EV) charging systems, expanding its coverage to include the latest technology.

In a joint statement, the Land Transport Authority and Enterprise Singapore said the Technical Reference 25 will be elevated to a Singapore Standard SS 722 effective 1 April.

The latest standard will “be expanded to include updated specifications and guidelines for the design, installation, maintenance and operation of EV charging systems in Singapore to enhance their safety and reliability.”

Amongst the key changes in SS 722 is the Electrical Safety and General Requirements to cater for new types of EV charging systems such as wireless charging stations, mobile charging systems, and battery charge and swap stations for four-wheeled vehicles.

Chargers that have already been type-approved under the EV Charging Act based on the earlier TR 25 standards do not need to be re-certified to the new SS standard. There will be a transition period of two and a half years before compliance with SS 722 becomes mandatory for new chargers.

“This elevation also supports the continued expansion of Singapore's charging network through standardised technical practices that ensure consistent quality across charging stations island-wide,” authorities said.

data.title: GIC elevates key investment heads to executive group
data.description:
To mark 45 years of investment excellence, the sovereign wealth fund has announced key leadership appointments.

GIC, Singapore’s sovereign wealth fund, has named two new investment heads to support its focus on long-term institutional continuity.

According to a statement, GIC appointed Mark Ong, chief investment officer, Public Equities, and Boon Chin Hau, chief investment officer, Infrastructure, to its highest management body, the Group Executive Committee.

“The leaders will play a critical role in charting GIC’s active investment strategies and fostering a culture of excellence,” the statement read.

The appointments, which coincided GIC’s 45th anniversary, are effective from 1 April.

GIC also announced the appointment of Bob Prince, co-chief investment officer, Bridgewater Associates & Chair of Bridgewater’s Board of Directors, to its International Advisory Board. He will also serve in an advisory capacity to GIC’s Investment Strategies Committee.

data.title: ST Engineering unit bags $600m naval deal
data.description:
The deal covers the design and platform supply for eight Missile Gun Boats.

ST Engineering’s marine unit has secured a six-year sub-contract valued at about $600m from Abu Dhabi Ship Building (ADSB).

Under the deal, the company’s marine arm will design and supply the platform systems for a fleet of eight Missile Gun Boats that ADSB is constructing for the Kuwait Naval Force. Three of the vessels will be built at ST Engineering’s Singapore shipyard.

This contract follows an earlier award in 2021 by ADSB for four similar Offshore Patrol Vessels for the UAE Navy.

The Missile Gun Boats will be based on ST Engineering’s proprietary Fearless-class patrol vessel, purpose-built for versatility and reliability.

The design features high redundancy for critical systems to maximise mission readiness and supports continuous operations. The vessels will also incorporate advanced platform systems that enhance navigation precision.

data.title: Microsoft commits $7.095b to Singapore’s AI push
data.description:
The funds will build out critical cloud and AI infrastructure through 2029.

Microsoft is committing $7.095b (US$5.5b) through 2029 to support Singapore’s artificial intelligence (AI) push.

“Our ongoing investment in cloud and AI infrastructure reflects Microsoft’s long-term confidence in Singapore as a global digital leader,” Brad Smith, vice chair and president of Microsoft, said in a statement.

“Together, we’re focused on helping people and organisations use AI by strengthening skills, increasing cybersecurity and resilience, and advancing trusted governance so technology delivers real benefits for Singaporeans,” he added.

According to Microsoft, the investment will be used in cloud and AI infrastructure and its ongoing operations in Singapore. Efforts to strengthen AI skills across sectors and communities, whilst increasing cybersecurity, resilience and trusted governance will also be continued, it added.

“By embedding AI literacy into everyday learning and in how every sector, from enterprise to nonprofits operate, we’re building on the National AI Strategy 2.0 to drive inclusive, trusted AI adoption for genuine impact,” said Wee Luen Chia, managing director, Microsoft Singapore.

US$1 = S$1.29

data.title: Boustead wins over $100m Tukang industrial contract
data.description:
Project lifts engineering backlog to $566m.

Boustead Projects has secured an engineering and construction contract worth more than $100m to build a 10-storey ramp-up industrial development at Tukang Innovation Drive in Singapore.

The Business 2 project sits under a 50:50 joint venture between Boustead Projects’ subsidiary, BP-Innovue, and Lian Beng Group’s subsidiary, SLB Development, which won the JTC land tender announced in May 2025.

The completed development will house more than 260 strata-titled industrial units designed for heavy industrial operations across an 18,687 square meter (sq m) site with an allowable gross floor area of 46,717.5 sq m.

It is planned as a multi-user facility with dedicated heavy vehicle parking and is expected to be completed within 24 months.

Boustead said the contract raised the Real Estate Solutions Division’s order backlog to $402m and the group’s engineering order backlog to $566m.

The company added that the contract is not expected to have a positive material impact on profitability, earnings per share, and tangible net asset value per share for the financial year ending 31 March 2026.

data.title: First REIT proposes $471.5m Indonesia asset divestment
data.description:
Deal includes a six-asset put option.

First REIT has proposed divesting Indonesian assets worth about $471.5m as part of a strategic review aimed at reducing currency volatility, cutting leverage, and repositioning the portfolio towards developed markets.

The proposed divestments comprise eight hospital assets to PT Siloam International Hospitals Tbk and its subsidiaries for about $389.2m, two non-hospital assets to PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk for $53.3m, and a prepaid lease of commercial rights for Lippo Plaza Kupang to a unit of PT Metropolis Propertindo Utama for $29.1m.

The manager has also secured put options over six remaining Indonesian hospital assets with an aggregate agreed property value of about $294.8m, which would allow First REIT to fully exit Indonesia if exercised. Any exercise would require a separate unitholder vote.

First REIT said the proposed divestments were struck at a 2.1% premium to valuation and would lower aggregate leverage to 16.7% after debt repayment, generating pro forma annual interest cost savings of $18.8m.

The board also intends to recommend a special distribution of about $9.7m, whilst the trust expects to recover $6.9m of current rental arrears from MPU in connection with completion.

The trust said the transactions would help eliminate rupiah-Singapore dollar foreign exchange drag on returns and recycle capital from non-core, non-healthcare, and arrears-linked assets.

It plans to focus future growth on developed Asia-Pacific markets, including Singapore, Japan, and Australia.

The proposed divestments remain subject to unitholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting expected in June, with completion targeted for August. The manager said the sponsors, OUE Limited and OUE Healthcare Limited, and their associates will abstain from voting.

data.title: Grab and WeRide launch first public autonomous ride service
data.description:
Punggol pilot offers free rides.

Grab and WeRide have launched Singapore’s first autonomous public ride service in Punggol, marking the first autonomous passenger service deployed in a residential estate.

The Ai.R service began public operations on 1 April and uses WeRide’s GXR and Robobus vehicle models.

The partners said the fleet had already served more than 1,000 trial riders and logged over 30,000km of autonomous mileage since January.

The service operates on weekdays from 9:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m., with riders able to choose between two full shuttle routes and a 20-minute mini route. Rides will remain free until commercial service begins in mid-2026.

Grab and WeRide said the launch has also created new roles for driver-partners, including Safety Operator and Remote Operator. They said 14 Grab driver-partners have completed training to become certified Safety Operators, with more undergoing training and assessments.

data.title: Jalan Buroh site launched under H1 2026 IGLS programme
data.description:
The site is the first of six Confirmed List plots.

JTC has launched a 3.12-hectare industrial site at Jalan Buroh under the first half of the 2026 Industrial Government Land Sales (IGLS) programme.

The site has a gross plot ratio of 2.5, is zoned B2, and comes with a 33-year tenure.

It is the first of six Confirmed List sites and forms part of 8.58 hectares of industrial land to be released via the Confirmed List in the first half of the 2026 IGLS programme.

Applications close on 26 May 2026 at 11:00 a.m. Interested parties can purchase the Tenderer’s Packet for $185.30, inclusive of GST.

data.title: MOM and MND launch Lok Yang Way dormitory site
data.description:
The site can yield 5,000 bedspaces.

The Ministry of Manpower and the Ministry of National Development have jointly released a purpose-built dormitory site at Lok Yang Way.

JTC launched the site as land sales agent for the 2.84-hectare parcel, which is zoned Civic and Community Institution and comes with a 30-year tenure.

The site can support up to 5,000 bedspaces and up to 1,000 square metres of commercial space.

Tender for the site closes on 23 June 2026 at 11:00 am. Interested parties can purchase the tender packet for $185.30, inclusive of GST.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI up 1.85%; Top stock is Jardine Matheson
data.description:
Jardine Matheson is the top stock with a 4.78% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 4,975.830, up 1.85% on Wednesday, 1 April, at 5:21 p.m.

Jardine Matheson was the most active stock, followed by Yangzijiang Shipbuilding (Holdings) Pte Ltd (+4.76%) and Hongkong Land (+4.25%).

Wilmar International and Genting Singapore Limited were at the foot.
